scope.target: Resolve D1-D10 before canonical repository rules are changed
constraints: Do not infer unresolved architecture direction
Record the reason and impact of each approved decision
Mark intentionally postponed decisions as deferred

inputs.architecture_target: Approved repository, query, persistence, transaction, deletion, and validation contract
review_findings: D1, D2, D4, D5, D6, D7, D8, D9, and D10 are resolved
D3 concerns multiple instances of the same Aggregate type, not multiple Aggregate types
D3 resolved to a minimal portable repository plus documented target-specific batch extension pattern
The current Use Case and Handler relationship is inconsistent with the user's target and requires a separate workflow
user_constraints: Avoid later correction errors by asking for decisions first
Do not prescribe database or persistence packages in the normative contract
Use conditional technology guidance for EF Core, Dapper, and other adapters
Defer translation and do not use translation sub-agents in this workflow
Keep IDomainRepository as an IAggregateRepository compatibility contract
Provide a separate restricted physical purge capability
Use explicit IUnitOfWork injection only for Use Cases declaring synchronous strong consistency
Require semantic repository markers with error-level analyzer coverage

steps: Record resolved D5, D7, D8, and D9 decisions
Present reframed D1-D4, D6, and D10 with explicit role boundaries, options, and tradeoffs
Record resolved D1, D2, D4, D6, and D10 decisions
Explain and confirm the D3 same-Aggregate-type batch loading and persistence rule
Record the Use Case and Handler boundary as a separate follow-up workflow
Update target direction and downstream task constraints
Mark deferred decisions and their blocked scope
validation: Every decision has an explicit state
Downstream tasks reference the approved direction
No contradiction remains between selected decisions

results.summary: Resolved D1-D10. The portable repository contract is aggregate-root-only and technology-neutral, IDomainRepository is a compatibility contract, batch persistence is a target-specific opt-in pattern, query access is read-only, explicit Unit of Work signals exceptional strong consistency, and semantic repository analyzer violations are errors.
tests_run: Parsed all workflow task JSON files
